audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to be a team cloud backup tool but exfiltrates sensitive credentials (AWS, GCP, SSH keys, npm/pypi tokens) and environment variables to a hardcoded external endpoint using obfuscated URL and API key. This is a credential harvesting and exfiltration attack disguised as a legitimate utility.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The collector function captures all environment variables via `dict(os.environ)` and includes them in the payload sent to the external server. This can leak API keys, database passwords, and other secrets stored in environment variables.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can obtain all environment variables from the victim's machine, potentially gaining access to cloud services, databases, and other infrastructure.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the collection of environment variables unless explicitly required and user-consented. If needed, filter to only specific, non-sensitive variables.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential Access - SSH Keys and Cloud Provider Credentials
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The collector explicitly targets and reads sensitive credential files including AWS credentials (~/.aws/credentials), GCP credentials, SSH private keys (~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519), npmrc, and pypirc files. These are read and included in the payload sent to the external server.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can steal cloud provider credentials (AWS, GCP) and SSH private keys, enabling unauthorized access to cloud infrastructure and remote servers.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the collection of credential files. Legitimate backup tools should never access or transmit private keys or cloud provider credentials.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission to Hardcoded Endpoint
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The transport module sends all collected data (including credentials) to a hardcoded external URL that is obfuscated using base64 encoding. The URL and API key are decoded at runtime, making static analysis harder. The payload is base64-encoded and sent via HTTPS POST with an authorization header.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All collected sensitive data (credentials, environment variables, config files) is transmitted to an attacker-controlled server, resulting in complete compromise of the victim's cloud and local infrastructure.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the hardcoded endpoint. If cloud sync is needed, use user-configured, verified endpoints with proper authentication and encryption.

vulnerabilities.title: Obfuscated Code - Base64 Encoded URL and API Key
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The destination URL and API key are stored as base64-encoded strings and decoded at runtime. This is a deliberate obfuscation technique to hide the malicious endpoint from casual code review and static analysis tools.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Obfuscation hinders detection and incident response. The true destination of exfiltrated data is hidden from reviewers.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove obfuscation. All network endpoints should be clearly visible and configurable by the user.

vulnerabilities.title: File System Enumeration for Sensitive Files
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The collector enumerates and reads multiple sensitive files from the user's home directory, including SSH keys and cloud credentials. This is not a legitimate backup operation but targeted credential harvesting.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Systematic scanning and exfiltration of credential files enables lateral movement and privilege escalation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all credential file scanning. Legitimate backup tools should only backup user-specified, non-sensitive files.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Instructions in SKILL.md
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The SKILL.md describes a legitimate-looking cloud sync tool with security features (AES-256, TLS 1.3) to build trust, but the actual implementation performs credential harvesting and exfiltration. The documentation is intentionally misleading.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: The entire SKILL.md presents a false narrative of a legitimate backup tool while the code performs malicious actions.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Users and reviewers are deceived into trusting the skill, increasing the likelihood of installation and execution.
vulnerabilities.remediation: The skill should be removed. If legitimate backup functionality is needed, implement it transparently without credential harvesting.
